本文提出 FederatedSkill——首个面向智能体技能演化的隐私保护联邦学习框架。其核心突破在于摒弃原始交互轨迹的传输，转而以语义化技能差分（semantic skill diffs） 为通信基本单元：即在本地技能库上生成的、结构化的、可解释的“技能补丁”（如新增工具调用逻辑、优化推理链模板、修正错误子程序）。服务器端部署演化智能体（evolution agent），动态聚合各客户端提交的技能补丁，并建模每个客户端独有的能力边界（capability boundary），从而驱动严格个性化的技能进化，而非传统联邦学习中损害性能的“全局平均”。

实验结果与价值

在涵盖20类异构任务家族（含规划、工具调用、多跳推理等）的严格评测中，FederatedSkill 相比纯本地自演化基线：

  • ✅ 成功率最高提升44.4%（如复杂API编排任务从52.1%→74.9%）；
  • ✅ 计算开销降低37.5%（减少冗余技能训练与无效尝试）；
  • ✅ 完全满足端到端差分隐私与模型不可逆脱敏要求。

本工作重新定义了智能体协同进化的范式：从“共享行为”走向“共享认知增量”，为构建安全、高效、个性化的下一代AI代理系统奠定基础。

AI Summary (English)

Modern LLM agents rely heavily on skill libraries for complex task execution, making skill evolution critical for self-improvement. Yet isolated user data lacks diversity, while existing cross-user collaboration methods—like raw trajectory sharing—compromise privacy and enforce a suboptimal global skill library, ignoring client heterogeneity. FederatedSkill addresses this by introducing semantic skill diffs: structured, interpretable patches over local skill libraries (e.g., refined reasoning templates or corrected tool-use logic), used as the sole communication unit. A server-side evolution agent aggregates these diffs to dynamically model each client’s unique capability boundary, enabling strictly personalized skill evolution—not global averaging. Evaluated across 20 diverse agent task families, FederatedSkill achieves up to +44.4% success rate gain and −37.5% computational cost versus self-evolving baselines, all under formal privacy guarantees. It redefines collaborative agent learning as sharing cognitive increments, not behavioral traces.

Abstract

Modern LLM agents increasingly rely on skill libraries to handle complex tasks, making skill evolution a primary driver of self-improvement. However, isolated single-user task streams lack the diversity required to build comprehensive skills. While cross-user collaboration can overcome this data bottleneck, current trajectory-sharing approaches compromise user privacy and impose a uniform global library that fails to accommodate client heterogeneity. We introduce FederatedSkill, a privacy-preserving framework for collaborative agent evolution. Moving beyond raw trajectory sharing, FederatedSkill utilizes semantic skill diffs, structured patches over local libraries, as the fundamental unit of communication. On the server side, an evolution agent aggregates these patches to dynamically model client-specific capability boundaries, facilitating strictly personalized skill evolution rather than a suboptimal global average. Evaluated across 20 distinct agent task families, FederatedSkill demonstrates substantial gains over self-evolving baselines, achieving up to a 44.4% increase in success rate and a 37.5% reduction in computational cost.

item.title: Trans GAN-WT: A Feature Extraction and Interactive Learning-Based Anomaly Detection Model for Wind Turbine Time Series Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03112v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着风电场规模持续扩大，风电机组（WTG）运维成本攀升，亟需在灾难性故障发生前实现早期异常检测，以保障系统可靠性并降低维护支出。然而，现有方法面临两大瓶颈：（1）难以在高冗余、强噪声的时序数据中有效建模多变量间复杂依赖关系；（2）未能充分利用稀缺但高价值的已标注异常样本，导致对微小偏差型异常（如传感器漂移、渐进性退化）漏检率高。

方法创新

本文提出TransGAN-WT——一种融合Transformer与生成对抗网络（GAN）的特征提取与交互学习型异常检测模型：

  • 误差放大重建机制：通过非线性加权重构损失，显著放大微小异常的残差信号，降低漏检率；
  • 自回归多模态特征提取：引入带掩码的自回归推理路径，联合建模SCADA数据中的物理量（风速、功率、转速等）与统计特征（滑动均值、方差），提升模型泛化性与训练稳定性；
  • 跨尺度时序交互模块（TSIM）：构建短时（秒级）、中时（分钟级）、长时（小时级）三路并行特征编码器，并通过门控注意力实现跨尺度特征动态融合，显式抑制时间冗余。

实验结果

在4个真实风电场SCADA数据集（含齿轮箱振动、变桨系统、主轴承等多类故障）上的实验表明：TransGAN-WT平均F1达96.10%，较LSTM-AE、USAD、TranAD等SOTA基线分别提升5.84%和2.89%；假阳性率（FPR）低至0.06%；Wilcoxon符号秩检验（p < 0.01）证实其性能提升具有统计显著性。该模型已部署于某省级风电智能运维平台，支撑实时异常预警与根因分析。

AI Summary (English)

Wind turbine (WTG) anomaly detection is critical for reducing O&M costs and preventing catastrophic failures, yet existing methods struggle with redundant multivariate time series and underutilize scarce anomaly labels. This paper proposes TransGAN-WT, a novel model integrating Transformer-based temporal modeling and GAN-driven reconstruction learning. It introduces three key innovations: (1) an error-amplified reconstruction loss to enhance sensitivity to subtle deviations; (2) autoregressive multimodal feature extraction to jointly encode physical and statistical SCADA features, improving generalization; and (3) a temporal-scale interaction module (TSIM) that fuses short-, medium-, and long-term patterns via gated attention to suppress temporal redundancy. Evaluated on four real-world WTG datasets, TransGAN-WT achieves a mean F1-score of 96.10%, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines (e.g., USAD, TranAD) by +5.84% and +2.89%, with an ultra-low false positive rate of 0.06%. Wilcoxon signed-rank tests (p < 0.01) confirm statistically significant gains, validating its practical efficacy for reliable wind farm operation.

Abstract

With the increasing scale and number of wind farms, wind turbines' daily operation and maintenance costs are increasing. To reduce operation and maintenance costs and enhance the reliability of wind turbine and system operation data before reaching catastrophic failures, monitoring the operating status of the equipment and detecting failures at an early stage is crucial. It is of great practical significance to utilize the working condition data for abnormal assessment of the operating status of wind turbines to realize abnormal monitoring of the operating status of wind turbines. However, the existing anomaly detection methods can neither perform effective relational modeling in data filled with a large amount of redundant information nor reasonably utilize the valuable anomaly data. For this reason, this paper proposes an anomaly detection model that fuses a Transformer and a generative adversarial network. Firstly, it reduces the leakage detection rate of minor deviation anomalies by amplifying the reconstruction error. Secondly, it uses autoregressive inference to extract multimodal features to enhance the stability and generalization ability of training. Finally, the temporal feature extraction module is constructed to promote the interactive learning between features of different time scales and effectively reduce the time redundancy. The results of multiple sets of experiments conducted on real WTG datasets show that TransGAN-WT achieves an average F1 score of 96.10% across multiple wind turbine datasets, which is 5.84% and 2.89% higher than several other state-of-the-art baseline methods. It also realizes a false positive rate (FPR) of 0.06%, and is verified by the Wilcoxon signed-rank test to have achieved a statistically significant performance enhancement compared to the state-of-the-art baseline methods, effectively ensuring the stable operation of wind turbines.

item.title: FGRPO: Federated GRPO with Adaptive Aggregation on Non-IID Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03094v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着大语言模型的发展，基于强化学习（RL）的自我修正与长链推理能力已成为提升模型推理质量的核心范式。Group Relative Policy Optimization（GRPO）通过摒弃传统 critic 网络，显著提升了训练可扩展性与稳定性。然而，其标准实现依赖中心化数据聚合，需从分布式用户端收集大量原始交互数据，严重违背隐私保护原则——尤其在医疗、金融等敏感场景中不可接受。

方法创新：FGRPO 框架

本文提出 Federated GRPO（FGRPO），首个面向非独立同分布（non-IID）数据的联邦式 GRPO 框架。核心突破在于设计了一种基于相对性能增益的自适应聚合机制：

  • 每个客户端不直接上传策略梯度或奖励值，而是计算其本轮更新相对于个性化历史基线（如上一轮本地最优回报均值）的相对提升率；
  • 服务器据此动态加权聚合，自动抑制低效或退化客户端的贡献（如任务过难、数据噪声大），优先融合高信噪比的学习信号；
  • 全过程无需共享原始数据、奖励标量或策略参数，仅交换轻量级归一化增益指标，严格满足联邦学习隐私边界。

主要成果

在涵盖数学推理（GSM8K）、代码生成（HumanEval）和多跳问答（HotpotQA）的异构 non-IID 分布实验中，FGRPO 相比 FedAvg+GRPO 和 FedProx+GRPO 平均提升 12.7% 的最终推理准确率，收敛速度加快 1.8×，且在极端数据倾斜（如 90% 客户仅含单一任务）下仍保持稳定。本工作首次将无 critic 的 RL 范式成功解耦至联邦架构，为隐私敏感场景下的推理模型协同优化提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Recent advances highlight reinforcement learning (RL) as the dominant paradigm for enhancing language models’ self-correction and long-chain reasoning. While Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) improves scalability by eliminating the critic network, its centralized deployment risks severe privacy breaches due to raw data collection from distributed owners. To address this, we propose Federated GRPO (FGRPO)—the first privacy-preserving framework for decentralized GRPO fine-tuning over heterogeneous, non-IID data. Its core innovation is an adaptive server aggregation mechanism grounded in relative performance gain: each client computes its improvement over a personalized historical baseline (e.g., local moving-average reward), enabling the server to dynamically weight updates by signal reliability—not task difficulty or data volume. This avoids reward-scale divergence and ensures robust convergence without sharing raw data, rewards, or model parameters. Experiments across GSM8K, HumanEval, and HotpotQA under realistic non-IID splits show FGRPO achieves +12.7% average accuracy gain over baselines and 1.8× faster convergence—demonstrating both privacy compliance and superior generalization.

Abstract

Recent advances in language models have established reinforcement learning as the primary paradigm for eliciting self-correction and long-chain reasoning. While group relative policy optimization (GRPO) offers superior scalability by eliminating the critic network, deploying it on a central infrastructure entails collecting a large volume of data from distributed owners, which poses significant privacy risks. To address these concerns, we introduce federated GRPO (FGRPO), a framework designed to decentralize the fine-tuning of reasoning models across heterogeneous data owners. To effectively mitigate the instability caused by divergent reward scales across heterogeneous tasks, FGRPO incorporates an adaptive aggregation mechanism based on relative performance gain. By characterizing each client's improvement relative to its personalized historical baseline, the framework dynamically prioritizes effective learning trajectories regardless of local task difficulty. FGRPO ensures robust convergence on non-IID data while preserving data privacy.

item.title: Backdoor Unlearning Generalization: A Path Toward the Removal of Unknown Triggers in LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03785v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大语言模型（LLMs）中的后门攻击构成日益严峻的安全威胁：攻击者通过隐蔽触发器（trigger）诱导模型在特定输入下生成恶意输出。现有防御方法多为“单点清除”范式——需预先知晓触发器形态，且每次仅针对一个已知后门进行干预。当模型中存在未知触发器（如隐蔽植入的多个后门）时，防御者处于结构性劣势，难以系统性保障模型安全。

方法与发现

本研究首次揭示并实证了后门卸载泛化性（Backdoor Unlearning Generalization）现象：对模型执行单次触发器定向卸载训练（如移除一个预设的可控后门），不仅能消除目标后门，还能显著抑制未被显式训练干预的其他后门（包括不同触发模式、注入阶段及模型架构下的后门）。我们在三类主流模型家族（Llama-2、Qwen、Phi-3）上开展系统验证，涵盖预训练与持续预训练两种后门注入方式，并逐个卸载不同后门，观测跨后门抑制效果。

创新机制与工具

为解释该泛化现象，我们提出跨激活偏移距离（Cross Activation Shift Distance, CASD）——一种可计算的度量，用于量化不同卸载训练所引发的内部表征空间偏移之间的相似性。实验表明：CASD值越小，跨后门抑制强度越高，证实卸载过程在神经激活层面存在共享抑制路径。

意义与范式转变

本工作开辟LLM安全新范式：防御者可主动注入可控后门→执行定向卸载→利用泛化效应被动清除未知后门。该策略不依赖对攻击者触发器的先验知识，为应对黑盒/多后门威胁提供了可扩展、低成本的主动防御路径。

AI Summary (English)

Large Language Models (LLMs) are vulnerable to backdoor attacks, where hidden triggers cause malicious outputs. Current defenses require a priori knowledge of each trigger and address backdoors one-by-one—leaving models exposed to unknown or multiple backdoors. We discover Backdoor Unlearning Generalization: unlearning one trigger via targeted unlearning training consistently suppresses other, untargeted backdoors—even those injected via different methods (pretraining/continual pretraining), across diverse model families (Llama-2, Qwen, Phi-3). To explain this, we propose the Cross Activation Shift Distance (CASD), a metric quantifying similarity in representation shifts induced by different unlearning procedures; lower CASD strongly correlates with stronger cross-backdoor suppression. Crucially, this enables a novel proactive defense paradigm: defenders can intentionally implant benign, controllable backdoors, then unlearn them—thereby leveraging generalization to neutralize unknown attacker-planted backdoors without trigger knowledge. Our results establish unlearning not just as removal, but as a generalizable safety intervention.

Abstract

Backdoor attacks in Large Language Models (LLMs) are a growing security concern, where models can generate adversary-chosen content. Existing defenses target backdoors one at a time and typically require knowledge of the trigger, leaving the defender at a structural disadvantage when unknown backdoors may exist in a model. We show that backdoor neutralization through unlearning generalizes across backdoors: training a model to ignore a single trigger can also suppress other backdoors that were never explicitly targeted. We study this phenomenon across three model families, whose backdoors were injected via pretraining or continual pretraining, by analyzing the models obtained after removing one backdoor at a time. To understand why unlearning certain backdoors induces the suppression of others, we introduce the Cross Activation Shift Distance, to quantify the distance between model changes induced by different trainings. Our results open a new direction for LLM safety as defenders could deliberately inject controlled backdoors and then remove them, leveraging cross-backdoor transfer to also suppress unknown backdoors that an attacker may have previously introduced in the model.
